temporal_description: A medium close-up shot captures a tense conversation between a young man and a young woman in a dimly lit, stone-walled room. The man, on the left, has shaggy brown hair and wears a dark, striped sweater. He holds a large, open book, gesturing with it as he speaks to the woman. The woman, on the right, has dark, damp-looking hair and is dressed in a dark hooded garment. She listens with a serious and slightly concerned expression. The background is dark and atmospheric, with a large, arched window visible behind the man, casting a cool, greenish light into the space. A watermark with Chinese characters, "风行网," is visible in the upper right corner of the frame. The camera remains steady, focusing on the interaction between the two characters. A young man speaks in a slightly questioning and insistent tone, as if trying to confirm a past statement. "Doesn't show up on the map, does it? You said that last year." A young woman responds, her voice firm and slightly defensive, "That's right, I did." The background is quiet, with no music or significant sound effects, placing all the focus on the tense dialogue between the two characters.
